name: python-django
description: Python and Django patterns, pitfalls, and best practices

observations.title: Use dataclasses or Pydantic for structured data
observations.narrative: Prefer @dataclass or Pydantic BaseModel over plain dicts for structured data. You get type hints, validation, and IDE support instead of stringly-typed key access.

observations.title: Mutable default arguments are shared across calls
observations.narrative: def f(items=[]): is a classic Python bug — the list is shared across all calls. Use def f(items=None): items = items or [] instead.

observations.title: Django select_related and prefetch_related prevent N+1
observations.narrative: Always use .select_related() for ForeignKey and .prefetch_related() for ManyToMany/reverse FK when accessing related objects in loops. Without them, each access fires a separate query.

observations.title: Use Django F() expressions for atomic field updates
observations.narrative: Model.objects.filter(pk=id).update(count=F('count') + 1) is atomic and avoids race conditions. Direct Python arithmetic (obj.count += 1; obj.save()) is not atomic.

observations.title: Python match/case for structural pattern matching
observations.narrative: Python 3.10+ match/case supports destructuring dicts, objects, and sequences. match response.status_code: case 200: ... is cleaner than if/elif chains for multi-branch logic.

observations.title: Use context managers for resource cleanup
observations.narrative: Always use 'with' statements for files, DB connections, locks. Write custom context managers with @contextmanager for any acquire/release pattern.

observations.title: Django timezone-aware datetime handling
observations.narrative: Use django.utils.timezone.now() not datetime.now(). Naive datetimes cause comparison bugs and warnings. Set USE_TZ=True in settings.

observations.title: Type hints with Protocol for duck typing
observations.narrative: Use typing.Protocol for structural (duck) typing instead of ABC. Protocols don't require inheritance — any class with matching methods satisfies the protocol.

observations.title: Django signals considered harmful for critical logic
observations.narrative: Avoid Django signals (post_save, pre_delete) for critical business logic. They run silently, are hard to debug, and create implicit coupling. Call service functions explicitly.

observations.title: Use __slots__ for memory-efficient value objects
observations.narrative: Add __slots__ = ('x', 'y') to classes with many instances. Eliminates per-instance __dict__, reducing memory by 40-50%. Tradeoff: no dynamic attributes.
